name: Ursuia's delights – running in the Basque Country
name_original: Les Délices d'Ursuia en off — Courir au Pays basque
description:
Les Délices d'Ursuia is a race of about 21 kilometers for 1,200 meters of elevation gain which has taken place at the beginning of April in Hasparren since 2007. Located at the foot of Mount Ursuia, it is one of the of the most extensive Basque Country with many scattered districts. Although its origins date back to the Roman Empire, Hasparren developed mainly through agriculture and the shoe industry.

The start is in the Urcuray district towards Cambo-les-Bains. The first five kilometers are almost exclusively uphill, until you reach the central element of the route: Mount Ursuia. As you approach its summit, you begin a small descent on a pretty path. The route meanders and oscillates on the sides of the mountain until it really reaches it after 14 kilometers.

From then on, you only have a slight descent left to complete your outing. This passes through the Iralmada pass before following a pretty little stream leading to your starting point.

The Ursu Mendi association has recently offered a shorter format over 12 kilometers with 500 meters of elevation gain, as well as a hike of around 15 kilometers.

You can find more information about the event here:
http://ursuiatrail.fr/
region_intro: Jogging routes around Hasparren offer a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by rolling pastures, moors, and hills. The region is nestled at the foot of Mont Ursuya, a prominent natural landmark under 700 meters, providing accessible routes with continuous panoramic vistas. Extensive areas of verdant meadows and agricultural land shape the terrain, offering a mix of gentle paths and more challenging ascents. Hasparren also features moors, a Natura 2000 zone, adding to the variety of running environments. Best jogging routes around Hasparren The most popular jogging route is Ursuia's delights – running in the Basque Country, a 13.4 miles (21.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 19 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers significant elevation gain through the Basque Country. Another top favourite among local runners is Balcon d'Ursuia – View of the Valley loop from Cimetière d'Urcuray, a difficult 10.9 miles (17.6 km) path. This route provides panoramic views of the surrounding valleys and Mont Ursuya. Local runners also love the Ridge line 🤩 – Mount Ursuia loop from Urcaray, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail leading through rolling pastures and moors, often completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes. Jogging around Hasparren is defined by Mont Ursuya, rolling pastures, and extensive moors. The network offers options for different ability levels, from gentle paths to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Hasparren are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 2300 runners have used komoot to explore Hasparren's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Hasparren?
A: Hasparren offers a wide array of options for runners, with over 70 distinct routes available. These trails cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy paths to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Hasparren?
A: The running routes in Hasparren are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 2300 runners have explored the area, frequently praising the diverse landscapes, continuous panoramic views of the Basque mountains, and the varied terrain around Mont Ursuya.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Hasparren?
A: Yes, Hasparren provides several options for beginners or those seeking a more relaxed run. There are 5 easy routes available, often winding through the verdant rolling pastures and meadows. These paths offer a gentle introduction to the region's natural beauty without significant elevation.

Q: Where can I find more challenging running trails in Hasparren?
A: For runners looking for a challenge, Hasparren has 21 difficult routes, many of which explore the slopes of Mont Ursuya. Notable challenging trails include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041293229/ursuia-s-delights-running-in-the-basque-country" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ursuia's delights – running in the Basque Country&lt;/a&gt;, a 21.5 km trail with significant elevation, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24732801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcon d'Ursuia – View of the Valley loop from Cimetière d'Urcuray&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views over 17.6 km.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Hasparren?
A: Yes, many of the running paths in Hasparren are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41172876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ridge line 🤩😍 – Mount Ursuia loop from Urcaray&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 12.4 km circular trail that takes you through rolling pastures and moors.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the running trails around Hasparren?
A: The trails around Hasparren offer diverse and picturesque scenery. You'll encounter verdant rolling pastures, extensive moors (including a Natura 2000 zone), and hilly landscapes. Many routes provide continuous panoramic vistas of the Basque and Bearn mountains, with Mont Ursuya serving as a prominent natural landmark.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions to see while running in Hasparren?
A: While running in Hasparren, you'll be surrounded by the natural beauty of the Basque Country. The region is known for its mountain peaks and passes. You might catch glimpses of peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1365446" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont Artzamendi&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2829786" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondarrain&lt;/a&gt;, and pass through areas near mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2648545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col de Pinodieta&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Iramalda&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Hasparren?
A: Many outdoor trails in the French Basque Country are dog-friendly, especially those through natural landscapes like pastures and moors. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, particularly in agricultural areas where sheep, horses, and cows are often present in fenced meadows. Check specific route details on komoot for any restrictions.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for families with children in Hasparren?
A: For families, the easier and shorter routes through the rolling pastures and moors are generally suitable. These paths offer a more relaxed experience, allowing children to enjoy the natural environment. Look for routes with minimal elevation gain and shorter distances for a comfortable family outing.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging in Hasparren?
A: Hasparren's diverse landscapes are enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended. Winter jogging is possible, but some higher elevation trails might be more challenging depending on weather conditions.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer particularly good viewpoints?
A: Absolutely! Many routes around Mont Ursuya are renowned for their magnificent viewpoints. For instance, the 'L'Ursuya depuis Urcuray' route (mentioned in regional research) provides a stunning 360° viewpoint over the Basque Coast and the Béarnese Pyrenees. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24732801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcon d'Ursuia – View of the Valley loop from Cimetière d'Urcuray&lt;/a&gt; also offer panoramic views of the surrounding valleys.

Q: Is there parking available near the running trailheads in Hasparren?
A: While specific parking information for every trailhead isn't always listed, Hasparren is a town with various access points to its extensive network of trails. Many routes, especially those starting from villages or specific points like 'Cimetière d'Urcuray' or 'Zelai', typically have nearby parking options. It's recommended to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more precise parking details.
tips: Beautiful trail on the side of Mount Ursuia. On a clear day, it is possible to see the Atlantic Ocean in the distance.
Mount Ursuia culminates at 681 meters above sea level. Its geographical location slightly set back from the chain offers a magnificent panorama of the Pyrenees as well as the Atlantic Ocean.
The Iramalda pass culminates at 397 meters above sea level. At the foot of Mount Ursuia it also offers a beautiful view of the Baïgura.
